name: Zaynab bint Ali
title: Aqilah of Bani Hashem
shortDescription: The courageous daughter of Imam Ali (AS) whose fiery speeches after Ashura shook the foundations of the Umayyad rule.
fullStory:
Zaynab al-Kubra (SA), the esteemed daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and Fatima al-Zahra (SA), is the third child of this heavenly family. Raised in the cradle of revelation, she possessed immense knowledge and eloquence from a young age, earning her the title 'Aqilah of Bani Hashem' (the wise lady of Bani Hashem). The deep emotional bond between Zaynab and her brother Imam Hussain (AS) was so strong that she made accompanying her brother a condition for her marriage to Abdullah ibn Ja'far.

During the tragedy of Ashura, Lady Zaynab (SA) was the pillar of the camp and the main support for Imam Hussain (AS) and the family. She was present through all the difficult moments in Karbala and, with mountain-like patience, witnessed the martyrdom of her brothers, nephews, and even her own two young sons (Awn and Muhammad). Despite this immense grief, she never neglected the care of the women, children, and Imam Sajjad (AS) who was severely ill, managing the crisis excellently amidst the burning tents.

According to the precise report of the book Al-Irshad written by Sheikh Mufid, after the martyrdom of Imam Hussain (AS) and the looting of the tents, Umar ibn Sa'd's army took the survivors and the household of the Prophet as captives. On this arduous journey, Zaynab (SA) defended the sanctity of the Ahl al-Bayt with Alawi firmness. When the caravan of captives entered Kufa, she delivered a speech so powerful and eloquent that it reminded everyone of the eloquence of her father Ali (AS), bringing the Kufans who had gathered to watch to tears and deep remorse.

Sheikh Mufid writes in Al-Irshad: When Ibn Ziyad arrogantly asked Zaynab (SA) in the governor's palace in Kufa, 'How did you see what God did to your brother and your family?', this heroic lady responded with unparalleled courage and certainty: 'I saw nothing but beauty (Ma ra'aytu illa jamila). They were a group for whom God had destined martyrdom, so they hastened to their resting places, and soon God will gather you and them together for judgment'.

The fiery and revealing speeches of Lady Zaynab (SA) in Kufa and later in Yazid's court in Damascus unmasked the hypocrisy of the Umayyads and turned their military victory into a disgraceful political and ideological defeat. Her courage and enlightenment ensured that the true message of the Ashura uprising remained eternal in history, and the unjustly spilled blood of the Karbala martyrs was never forgotten.
quote: I saw nothing but beauty (in what God destined for us).
